$comment: Single source of truth for per-feature stability. Schema and consumers: docs/feature-status.md. statusDefinitions are the canonical user-facing explanations (pill tooltips in the app and on the website). All notes are reviewed by David; keep them honest, one line, and free of "I"/"we" (website voice).

statusDefinitions.alpha: Fresh feature. Should work. Might be broken.
statusDefinitions.beta: Works, but expect rough edges and smaller bugs.
statusDefinitions.stable: Well-tested, mature in the context of the whole app.
statusDefinitions.planned: Not built yet. See the roadmap for timing.

features.name: Search
features.note: Finds files fast, but the AI query translation still misreads some prompts and a few filters are rough.

features.name: Select files dialog
features.note: Wildcard and regex matching work well; AI selection is cloud-only and still misses edge cases.

features.name: Image search
features.note: Search the things (dogs, sunset, document, etc., and also text via OCR) inside your photos and screenshots. Runs on your Mac, fully local.

features.name: Archives
features.note: Browse, create, edit, and extract zip, tar, and 7z archives like folders, including password-protected ones.

features.name: Operation log
features.note: A durable history of every copy, move, delete, and rename, with rollback. The dialog that shows it is still bare-bones.

features.name: Ask Cmdr
features.note: Chat with an AI agent about your files. It reads names, paths, sizes, and history, plus any files you attach, and shows what each reply costs. Early, with rough edges. Read-only for now. On-device or Cloud via API key, your choice.

features.name: Network drives (SMB)
features.note: Browsing and fast direct transfers work on common NAS setups; unusual server configs can still cause surprises.

features.name: Android devices (MTP)
features.note: Fast transfers on most devices, but macOS sometimes claims the device first and some phones need a reconnect.

features.name: Git browser
features.note: Browse history, branches, tags, and stashes like folders. Very large repos can take a moment to load.

features.name: Local AI
features.note: The on-device model runs fully private on Apple Silicon; being a small model, it sometimes gives odd suggestions.

features.name: Download notifications
features.note: Catches downloads and jumps to the latest with one key; some browsers' temp-file renames can still slip through.

features.name: Go to path
features.note: Paste or type any path and jump there; it's new, so recents and edge-case path handling are still settling.

features.name: Dual-pane file explorer
features.note: Two panes, virtual scrolling through 100k+ files, live file watching, and Quick Look. The heart of the app.

features.name: File operations
features.note: Copy, move, delete, rename, and new folders with progress, conflict handling, and rollback. The most-tested code in the app.

features.name: File viewer
features.note: Opens even 10+ GB files instantly, with search and word wrap.

features.name: Tabs
features.note: Per-pane tabs with pinning, history, and reopen closed tab.

features.name: Command palette
features.note: Fuzzy-find every action with ⌘⇧P.

features.name: Keyboard shortcuts
features.note: Customize any shortcut, with conflict detection and scope awareness.

features.name: Full-disk index
features.note: Indexes your whole drive once, then stays current through file system events. Powers search and folder sizes.

features.name: Drag and drop
features.note: Drag files in, out, and between panes, including to and from Finder and other apps.

features.name: AI rename
features.note: Rename files by describing what you want, with a full preview before anything changes.

features.name: AI file organization
features.note: Say "clean up my Downloads folder" and approve or reject each suggested move.

features.name: More file systems
features.note: S3 buckets, FTP(S), SFTP, WebDAV, NFS, and more.

features.name: Tell me about this
features.note: Right-click any file for a quick AI explanation of what it is.

features.name: Plugins
features.note: Extend Cmdr with your own scripts and tools.

features.name: Disk space visualizer
features.note: A built-in treemap to see what's eating your disk.

features.name: Windows and Linux support
features.note: Bring Cmdr to more platforms.
